title: davallia assamica
intro: Davallia assamica is a type of fern, specifically a species of the Davallia genus, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia and Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Davallia assamica include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and poultices, which are made by steeping the leaves or roots in hot water.
